Lender's sales agents sent repeated promotional SMS and made calls to a person who had never consented or shared data with the company. Company initially denied agent was affiliated with them; ODPC confirmed the agent was a Platinum Credit representative.
determinations.prosecution_note: ODPC recommended prosecution of directors under s.57(3) read with s.73 DPA — up to KES 3M fine, 10 years imprisonment, or both.

Used complainant's image on social media and physical banners at events without consent and without informing the data subject.

determinations.respondent: Bolt Operations OU / Bolt Support Kenya Limited
determinations.violation_summary: Bolt's outsourced customer support agent improperly processed account change requests without adequate identity verification, enabling fraudsters to take over a driver's account and conduct 17 fraudulent trips worth KES 26,250. Bolt failed to report the breach within 72 hours and had not conducted a DPIA for its account management systems in Kenya.

determinations.complainant_category: Individual (parent suing on behalf of minor)
determinations.violation_summary: School used minor's image in billboard and website advertising without obtaining parental consent, failed to prove consent was granted.

violation_type: Failure to honour objection to processing
determinations.violation_summary: Digital lender collected personal data of third-party emergency contact (the complainant) without obtaining her consent, then persisted in contacting her despite her objection.

Complainant alleged LemonCash contacted him about a loan he was not party to. ODPC investigation found no evidence the mobile numbers belonged to LemonCash. Complaint dismissed. However, LemonCash denied investigators access to its database backend during site visit, constituting obstruction of the Data Commissioner under Section 61.

violation_type: Failure to honour right to erasure
Continued processing after withdrawal of consent
determinations.violation_summary: Former employee requested erasure of his images from respondent's Facebook page after employment ended. Despite several requests and consent withdrawal, respondent failed to remove all images even after ODPC complaint notification.

violation_type: Failure to respond to deletion request
determinations.violation_summary: Hospital used complainant's image on its social media page without consent, passing him off as an employee. Respondent failed to respond to the notification of complaint and continued using the image for over a year despite the complainant's demand for removal. Found liable for commercial use of personal data without consent.

violation_type: Alleged unlawful disclosure of bank account details
determinations.violation_summary: Complainant alleged Prime Bank disclosed his joint bank account details to third parties without consent. Bank disclosed details to executors of a deceased account holder pursuant to a High Court grant of probate. ODPC found the bank had a lawful basis under Section 30(1)(b) -- compliance with legal obligation. Complaint dismissed.
